entity: Pizza Hut Guarantor, LLC and Subsidiary

note_families.title: Description of Business
key_policies: PH Guarantor formed Nov 30, 2016 as single-member Delaware LLC
Guarantees performance of PHLLC under franchise registrations and agreements
PH LLC contributed to Company Jan 23, 2017 at carrying value of $24.4M
Required to maintain minimum net worth of $15M for large franchisor exemption
Member's equity $69.1M at Dec 29, 2025 — exceeds requirement

note_families.title: Principles of Consolidation
key_policies: Consolidated financials include Company and subsidiary PHLLC
Intercompany eliminated
52 weeks for 2025 and 2023; 53 weeks for 2024
VIE assessment: franchisee entities not consolidated; PHLLC not primary beneficiary
AdCom (advertising cooperative) consolidated due to PHLLC voting control over spending
Digico LLC and QuikOrder LLC not consolidated (no controlling interest)
PH Connect LLC: Assignment and Assumption Agreement (end of FY2019) transferred restaurant-level tech services from PHLLC to PH Connect

note_families.title: Fiscal Year
key_policies: 52-week fiscal calendar ending Monday before YUM's fiscal year end
53rd week every 5-6 years; FY2024 had 53rd week
53rd week added $5.5M revenue and $4.0M net income in FY2024
Next 53rd week: 2030

note_families.title: Cash, Restricted Cash
key_policies: Restricted cash: <$0.1M (2025), $16.0M (2024) — advertising dues from franchisees
Cash exceeded FDIC insured amount at Dec 29, 2025
No losses experienced in bank accounts

note_families.title: Accounts Receivable
key_policies: Generated from ongoing franchise relationships including AdCom contributions
Generally due within 30 days
Allowance considers current conditions and reasonable forecasts
Bad debt expense: $10.7M (2025), $10.1M (2024), $(0.9)M recovery (2023) — franchise/license
Bad debt expense: $7.2M (2025), $11.3M (2024), $0.1M (2023) — advertising
Allowance balance: $13.1M (2025), $22.9M (2024)

note_families.title: Software and Computer Equipment
key_policies: Capitalizes application development stage internal use software costs
Useful lives: 3-7 years
Amortization recorded in Franchise and license expenses
E-commerce/digital services via Digico (not consolidated)

note_families.title: Revolving Line of Credit
key_policies: AdCom party to $30M revolving LOC with commercial bank

note_families.title: Revenue Recognition
key_policies: Franchise rights granted through store-level franchise agreements
Continuing fees: 4-6% of restaurant sales, billed/paid monthly — recognized when sales occur (sales-based royalty exception under Topic 606)
Upfront franchise fees: recognized over term of franchise agreement on straight-line basis (interrelated with franchise right, not individually distinct)
Advertising: PHLLC acts as principal; advertising services not distinct from franchise right; recognized when franchise sales occur
Other services (technology, supply chain, QA): individually distinct from franchise right; recognized as goods/services transferred; PHLLC acts as principal

note_families.title: Franchise Support Costs
key_policies: Direct franchise costs → Franchise and license expenses
Unreimbursed support costs → G&A
Reimbursed expenses → Franchise advertising and other services expense
Advertising expense accrued at year-end if ad revenues exceed expenses (obligated to spend)

note_families.title: Franchise Incentives
key_policies: Cash or other incentives to drive new development or same-store growth
Capitalized as Franchise incentives on balance sheet
Amortized as reduction in Franchise and license revenues over expected cash flow period

note_families.title: Contract Liabilities (Deferred Franchise Fees)
key_policies: Unamortized upfront fees from franchisees
Includes deposits for stores not yet opened: $1.7M (2025), $1.6M (2024)

note_families.title: Income Taxes
key_policies: Both PH Guarantor and PHLLC are single-member LLCs disregarded for tax
Not subject to U.S. federal and state income taxes
Income taxed at YUM level
No current or deferred income taxes recorded

note_families.title: Related Party Transactions and Cost Allocations
key_policies: Neither Company nor PHLLC have employees
All resources provided by PHA (Pizza Hut of America, LLC)
All PHA expenses (except restaurant/real estate specific) allocated to Company financials
No management services agreement between PHA and Company/PHLLC
No YUM costs allocated
PHA royalties to PHLLC reflected as Due from affiliate
Cash settled daily with YUM (except restricted cash)
Due from YUM in equity represents cash sweep expected as noncash distribution

note_families.title: Member's Equity
key_policies: Single class of LLC interest
Initial contributions: $15M (Nov 30, 2016) + $3M (Jan 23, 2017) = $18M
$18M returned to Pizza Hut Holdings in 2018 after meeting net worth requirement
Due from YUM settled as noncash distribution

note_families.title: Total Costs and Expenses
key_policies: G&A breakdown: Personnel $39.8M, Professional/legal $7.1M, Travel $1.8M, D&A $16K, Software $65K, Other $5.9M = $54.7M total
Franchise/license: Discretionary marketing $3.5M, Customer experience $1.9M, Food safety $3.7M, Bad debt $10.7M, Other $3.3M = $23.1M total
Franchise advertising: Advertising $210.7M, Bad debt $7.2M, Overflow call center $5K, Other $1.0M = $218.9M total

note_families.title: Subsequent Events
key_policies: Evaluated through March 25, 2026 (issuance date)
No items to disclose

lease_accounting.note: No lease accounting disclosed — entity does not hold leases directly. PHALLC leases real estate to franchisees.
